issue.title: Markdown rendering in issue body and comments

successCriteria.criterion: Issue body in IssueDetail renders as GitHub Flavored Markdown (headings, bold, italic, lists, task lists, tables, strikethrough, autolinks)
Code blocks in both issue body and comments have syntax highlighting with a dark theme
Clickable links in rendered markdown open in the user's default browser (not inside the Tauri webview)
Inline images from GitHub render correctly with max-width constraint
Raw HTML in markdown is not rendered (react-markdown default behavior), preventing XSS
Markdown styles for issue body visually match the existing comment body markdown styles
pnpm typecheck passes with no errors

phases.title: Add markdown rendering to issue body and shared markdown CSS
verification: Issue body renders markdown (headings, lists, bold, code blocks, links, images)
Comments still render markdown correctly with the shared styles
GFM features work: tables render with borders, task lists show checkboxes, strikethrough renders, URLs auto-link
No duplicate CSS styles between IssueComments.css and markdown.css
white-space: pre-wrap is removed from .detail-body so markdown paragraphs render properly

phases.title: Add syntax highlighting for code blocks
verification: Fenced code blocks with language tags (```js, ```rust, ```python, etc.) display with syntax coloring
Code blocks without language tags still render correctly (auto-detection or plain text fallback)
The highlight theme looks appropriate in the dark UI (no white backgrounds, readable colors)
Both issue body and comments use the shared MarkdownRenderer component

phases.title: Handle external link clicks to open in default browser
verification: Clicking a link in the issue body markdown opens the URL in the OS default browser
Clicking a link in a comment markdown opens the URL in the OS default browser
Links have visual hover state (underline) from the markdown.css styles
Images from GitHub render inline within the markdown content
The Tauri webview does not navigate away when a link is clicked
